description.makeNewTools: The "Module" feature allows making new components by combining, specializing, or reparametrizing components created by existing tools in this simulator.
description.appearAsTools: Imported modules will appear as tools in the "Other" category. Please select and click the blank space according to the order of the control point sequence (see "Specification").
description.embeddedInScene: Imported modules are embedded in the data of the scene, so will not be automatically updated when the authors of the modules update them.

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Modules can be made or customized directly in the sidebar of the web app (see Settings -> Show sidebar). Customization of imported modules will only affect the current scene. Use "Import from file" below to import custom modules from another scene.

contribution.title: Contribution
contribution.description: Contributing to the list above is welcome. To contribute your module, see [Contributing modules](/contributing/modules).

FresnelLens.title: Fresnel Lens
FresnelLens.description: A Fresnel lens made from a half circle piece of glass. Modularized version of [this Gallery example](/gallery/fresnel-lens).
FresnelLens.point1: The first endpoint of the lens
FresnelLens.point2: The second endpoint of the lens
FresnelLens.refIndex: The refractive index of the lens

ContSpectrum.title: Continuous spectrum light source
ContSpectrum.description: A light source with a uniform continuous spectrum discretized with a given constant step. Only works when "Simulate Colors" is on.
ContSpectrum.point2: Determines the direction of the rays

RainbowPointSource.title: Rainbow Point Source
RainbowPointSource.description: Point light source <360 degrees - but emits a spectrum of colors. Must run with "Simulate Colors" on.

CoatedLens.title: Coated Spherical Lens
CoatedLens.description: A spherical lens with single-layer coatings on both sides. The `ArcCoating` module embedded in this module represents a single thin-film layer, which when overlapping with a glass' surface becomes its coating.
CoatedLens.R1: The radius of curvature of the front surface
CoatedLens.R2: The radius of curvature of the back surface
CoatedLens.A: The Cauchy coefficient \(A\) of the glass
CoatedLens.B: The Cauchy coefficient \(B\) of the glass in square micrometers
CoatedLens.dc: The thickness of the coating layer in nanometers
CoatedLens.Ac: The Cauchy coefficient \(A\) of the coating material
CoatedLens.Bc: The Cauchy coefficient \(B\) of the coating material in square micrometers

CircleSource.title: Circular light source
CircleSource.description: A circle with uniform 180-degree point sources places along its circumference.

EllipticalMirror.title: Elliptical Mirror
EllipticalMirror.description: An elliptical mirror defined by the two foci and the length of the semi-major axis.
EllipticalMirror.a: The length of the semi-major axis
EllipticalMirror.tMin: The minimum angle of the curve
EllipticalMirror.tMax: The maximum angle of the curve
EllipticalMirror.tStep: The step angle of the curve

Chaff.title: Chaff
Chaff.description: A chaff of a rectangular shape consisting of random pieces of mirrors. Modularized version of [this Gallery example](/gallery/chaff-countermeasure).
Chaff.point1: The top-left corner of the chaff
Chaff.point2: The bottom-right corner of the chaff
Chaff.N: The number of mirrors in the chaff

BeamExpander.title: Beam Expander
BeamExpander.description: The combination of two ideal lenses with the sum of their focal lengths equals their separation distance. They expand or reduce the diameter of a beam of collimated light. Modularized version of [this Gallery example](/gallery/beam-expanders).
BeamExpander.point1: The center of the first lens
BeamExpander.point2: The second point of the second lens
BeamExpander.point3: Determines the common focal point of the lenses

RayRelay.title: Ray Relay
RayRelay.description: A series of ideal identical lenses with focal length \(f\) and distance \(d\) between the lenses. A non-divergent ray trajectory is guaranteed if and only if \(d\leq 4f\). Modularized version of [this Gallery example](/gallery/ray-relaying).
RayRelay.point1: The first endpoint of the first lens
RayRelay.point2: The second endpoint of the first lens (should have the same \(x\) as the first point)
RayRelay.f: The focal length of the lenses

OpticalFiber.title: Optical Fiber
OpticalFiber.description: Line-shaped optical fiber with given core and cladding thickness and refractive indices.
OpticalFiber.point1: The first endpoint of the optical fiber
OpticalFiber.point2: The second endpoint of the optical fiber
OpticalFiber.n1: The refractive index of the core
OpticalFiber.n2: The refractive index of the cladding

CubeBeamSplitter.title: Cube Beam Splitter
CubeBeamSplitter.description: Cube beam splitter is a commonly used optical component. This is a cube beam splitter module that can be adjusted in size and angle.
CubeBeamSplitter.angle: The rotation angle of the cube in degrees
CubeBeamSplitter.refIndex: The refractive index of the cube (or Cauchy coefficient \(A\) when "Simulate Colors" is on)
CubeBeamSplitter.cauchyB: The Cauchy coefficient \(B\) of the cube in square micrometers
CubeBeamSplitter.splitRatio: The transmission ratio of the diagonal beam splitter
